summary: Verified all 5 new features + regression for the Wife To Be app. Backend pytest 15/15 PASS covering: (1) branded HTML render_email() path — no crash on request; (2) 'How did you hear about us?' question auto-seeded on both Warrington & Runcorn with id 'source'; (3) GET /api/analytics returns total/by_weekday(7)/by_hour/by_shop/by_source/completed/no_show/no_show_rate, filters by ?shop_id; (4) PATCH /api/bookings/{id} accepts status='no_show' and status filter ?status=no_show works; (5) POST /api/bookings/{id}/follow-up creates a linked booking sharing series_id (parent back-filled), GET /api/bookings/{id}/series returns >=2 linked bookings, invalid slot -> 409, missing parent -> 404; (6) GET /api/customers groups by email with totals + search filter (empty on no match), GET /api/customers/{email} returns full history (with answers persisted, including source='Instagram'), 404 for unknown email; regression: confirm/complete/cancel PATCH + CSV export still work with correct headers and content-type. Frontend UI verified end-to-end via Playwright: login OK via Sign In button; /admin/analytics shows KPI cards (an-total/an-completed/an-noshow/an-noshow-rate) and 4 bar charts (chart-weekday/hour/shop/source) plus per-shop filter; /admin/customers lists 17 grouped customers, search filter narrows to 1 for 'test_iter9', customer-history modal opens with source answer visible; /admin/bookings modal exposes noshow-booking button (purple), status filter includes 'no-show' option, add-follow-up reveals follow-up-form with label/type/date/time inputs and cancel-follow-up works; booking-answers section renders customer's source answer.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py analytics endpoint uses datetime.now().date() (naive local) — same tz caveat as iteration_8 report. Not a functional blocker.
GET /api/customers scans ALL bookings (up to 20000) in memory each call; consider MongoDB aggregation ($group by customer_email) once dataset grows.
GET /api/analytics also loads up to 20000 bookings in memory; fine at current scale but would benefit from aggregation pipeline.
server.py is now 1405 lines — earlier suggestion to split into routers stands (auth, shops, bookings, calendar, analytics, customers).
POST /bookings/{id}/follow-up does not enforce a shop-scoped permission for non-superadmins — same broader issue flagged in iteration_8 for CSV export.
seed_data_creation: Test booking created for TEST_Iter9 Customer / test_iter9@example.com with a follow-up in the same series. Cleanup routine cancels all such bookings at end of module (bookings become status='cancelled', not deleted).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Testids for new pages: analytics-shop, an-total, an-completed, an-noshow, an-noshow-rate, chart-weekday/hour/shop/source; customer-search, customers-table, customer-row-<email>, customer-modal, customer-history, close-customer-modal; add-follow-up, follow-up-form, fu-label, fu-type, fu-date, fu-time, save-follow-up, cancel-follow-up, booking-series, noshow-booking, booking-answers. Public booking flow's 'How did you hear about us?' dropdown is rendered from shop.questions where q.id=='source' (seeded on startup for existing shops). Follow-up creation validates availability via _compute_slots — pick a slot from GET /api/public/slots to guarantee 200; any arbitrary time returns 409. Email SMTP is intentionally not configured so all outbound is logged and returns False (not a bug).
rca of the issue: No product bugs found this iteration.
